chunks.content: ACUTE INFLAMMATION SUMMARY: Rapid response (minutes/hours) to injury delivering host defense (leukocytes/proteins) to the site. Key components: (1) Vasodilation (increased flow), (2) Permeability changes (edema), (3) Leukocyte emigration/activation.

Vascular events in acute inflammation are dominated by histamine-induced vasodilation and endothelial contraction, leading to increased permeability. This exudation of protein-rich fluid results in the clinical sign of edema (tumor).

IRON DEFICIENCY ANEMIA SUMMARY: Most common worldwide cause of anemia. Pathophysiology: Dietary lack, impaired absorption, or chronic blood loss. Morphology: Microcytic hypochromic erythrocyte appearance. Key labs: Low serum ferritin (<15µg/L).
